mainBoard.name: Goblin Dark-Dwellers
mainBoard.originalText:
Menace (This creature can't be blocked except by two or more creatures.)
When Goblin Dark-Dwellers enters, you may cast target instant or sorcery card with mana value 3 or less from your graveyard without paying its mana cost. If that spell would be put into your graveyard, exile it instead.

rulings.text: If an instant or sorcery card you cast this way goes to a zone other than exile or a graveyard, perhaps because one of its abilities says to put it into its owner's hand, it won't be exiled. This is true even if the card would be put into a graveyard later that turn.
If an instant or sorcery card you cast this way is countered, it will be exiled.
If the instant or sorcery card has {X} in its mana cost, you must choose 0 as its value.
If you can't cast the target instant or sorcery card, perhaps because there are no legal targets available, or if you choose not to cast it, it will remain in your graveyard.
If you cast a card "without paying its mana cost," you can't pay alternative costs such as surge costs. You can pay additional costs. If the card has mandatory additional costs, you must pay those.

You cast the card during the resolution of the enters-the-battlefield ability, not later in the turn. If it's a sorcery card, ignore the timing restrictions based on it being a sorcery card. Other timing restrictions, such as "Cast [this card] only during combat," must be followed.

rulings.text: An effect that changes a permanent's colors overwrites all its old colors unless it specifically says "in addition to its other colors." For example, after Cerulean Wisps resolves, the affected creature will just be blue. It doesn't matter what colors it used to be (even if, for example, it used to be blue and black).
Changing a permanent's color won't change its text. If you turn Wilt-Leaf Liege blue, it will still affect green creatures and white creatures.
Colorless is not a color.

rulings.text: If a card or token enters as a copy of a permanent, the new permanent isn't kicked, even if the original was.
If a spell's kicker cost was paid, the spell is "kicked."
If you copy a kicked spell on the stack, the copy is also kicked. If the copied spell is a permanent spell, the token the copy of that spell becomes when it enters is also kicked.
If you put a permanent with a kicker ability onto the battlefield without casting it, you can't kick it.
The kicker ability doesn't let you pay a kicker cost more than once.

To determine a spell's total cost, start with the mana cost (or an alternative cost if another card's effect allows you to pay one instead), add any cost increases (such as kicker), then apply any cost reductions. The spell's mana value remains unchanged, no matter what the total cost to cast it was.

иҲҲйҡҶгҒҷгӮӢзө¶еЈҒгҒҜгӮҝгғғгғ—зҠ¶ж…ӢгҒ§жҲҰе ҙгҒ«еҮәгӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒҢжҲҰе ҙгҒ«еҮәгӮӢгҒ«йҡӣгҒ—гҖҒиөӨгҒ§гҒӘгҒ„иүІпј‘иүІгӮ’йҒёгҒ¶гҖӮ
{T}пјҡ{R}гҒӢгҖҒгҒқгҒ®йҒёгҒ°гӮҢгҒҹиүІгҒ®гғһгғҠпј‘зӮ№гӮ’еҠ гҒҲгӮӢгҖӮ

foreignData.flavorText: A medida que pasaban los siglos, la roca se iba erosionando, revelando asГӯ los bellos colores del corazГіn de la montaГұa.
foreignData.name: Risco floreciente
foreignData.text:
El Risco floreciente entra girado. En cuanto entre, elige un color que no sea rojo.
{T}: Agrega {R} o un manГЎ del color elegido.

mainBoard.name: Thriving Bluff
mainBoard.originalText:
Thriving Bluff enters tapped. As it enters, choose a color other than red.
{T}: Add {R} or one mana of the chosen color.
